comments: Approved. The contract scope is tightly bounded to producing a single evidence-based assessment artifact, tasks/v0.7-m6-a-personal-pilot-report.md, evaluating how the V0.7 artifact taxonomy and lifecycle operate under Personal Mode for a single developer using an AI coding environment. This matches the recon packet's stated objective, analytical scope, and expected output, which explicitly frame M6-A as a documentation-based pilot rather than an implementation milestone. The out_of_scope clause is correctly restrictive: it forbids .agent-state physical migrations, MCP server modifications, new review automation code, expansion to formal rollouts, and active workflow state model changes, all of which are consistent with broader V0.7 requirements and prior roadmap documents that treat those concerns as separate or deferred work. The allowed_files and required_outputs lists remain aligned with the bounded reporting deliverable and review artifacts, and the stop conditions further prevent scope leakage into unauthorized files or automation implementation. No contract ambiguity or unauthorized execution surface was detected.
